title: Skeletal Animation Reproduction
summary: Rig and animate the staged character in Blender so it reproduces the target body motion from the reference video.
taskPrompt:
You are a 3D artist using Blender.

Your task is to rig and animate the provided character so that it reproduces the body motion shown in the reference video.

Official input:
- Unrigged mesh: `skeletal_animation_reproduction_singing_anime_character\input\Singing.obj`
- Material sidecar: `skeletal_animation_reproduction_singing_anime_character\input\Singing.mtl`
- Reference animation video: `skeletal_animation_reproduction_singing_anime_character\input\reference.mp4`

Required submission:
- Save a Blender scene named `final.blend` to `skeletal_animation_reproduction_singing_anime_character\output\submission\final.blend`
- Render and save a matching preview video named `preview.mp4` to `skeletal_animation_reproduction_singing_anime_character\output\submission\preview.mp4`

Required bone naming:
Your armature MUST use the following bone names exactly (case-sensitive):
  pelvis, spine, chest, neck, head,
  clavicle.L, upper_arm.L, lower_arm.L, hand.L,
  clavicle.R, upper_arm.R, lower_arm.R, hand.R,
  thigh.L, shin.L, foot.L,
  thigh.R, shin.R, foot.R
You may add extra bones, but all 19 listed above must be present with these exact names.

Notes:
- Focus on body motion.
- Evaluation will compare your `preview.mp4` against a hidden clean reference and also replay your `.blend`.
- Your `.blend` must contain the actual rig and animation that produce the submitted preview.
agentMustDo: Save a Blender scene named `final.blend` to `E:\agenthle\visual_media\skeletal_animation_reproduction\skeletal_animation_reproduction_singing_anime_character\output\submission\final.blend`

Render and save a matching preview video named `preview.mp4` to `E:\agenthle\visual_media\skeletal_animation_reproduction\skeletal_animation_reproduction_singing_anime_character\output\submission\preview.mp4`

Use all required bone names exactly as listed in the task prompt: pelvis, spine, chest, neck, head, clavicle.L, upper_arm.L, lower_arm.L, hand.L, clavicle.R, upper_arm.R, lower_arm.R, hand.R, thigh.L, shin.L, foot.L, thigh.R, shin.R, foot.R.
Evaluation will compare your `preview.mp4` against a hidden clean reference and also replay your `.blend`.
Your `.blend` must contain the actual rig and animation that produce the submitted preview.

outputContracts.name: Required bone naming
outputContracts.description: Agent-visible exact bone names required by the evaluator's skeleton coverage gate.

evaluation:
- The evaluator first validates that both submitted artifacts exist and that the Blender scene contains a usable animated rig with non-trivial motion.
- It then renders canonical replay evidence from `final.blend`, compares the replay and preview against the hidden clean reference video, measures required-bone coverage from `reference/package.json`, and uses multiple binary `YES` / `NO` vision questions to derive `vlm_score` as a yes-ratio.
- Final score is:

```text
if validity_gate fails:
    final_score = 0
else:
    final_score =
      0.35 * video_match_score +
      0.20 * replay_consistency_score +
      0.30 * minimal_skeleton_score +
      0.15 * vlm_score
```

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env

classifier.rationale: The task involves rigging and animating a character in a 3D software environment.
